metadata.context: Python library for human-friendly date and time handling.

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No audit trail for library build and release operations. No artifact signing or provenance attestation.
top_gaps.impact: Cannot verify integrity of published packages or trace release actions.

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.gap: No OpenTelemetry instrumentation; library operations are opaque in consumer traces.
top_gaps.impact: Consuming applications cannot attribute latency to Arrow operations.

top_gaps.title: Compute Hardening and Patching
top_gaps.gap: No Python dependency vulnerability scanning; Dependabot covers only GitHub Actions.
top_gaps.impact: Vulnerable dependencies could be published to downstream consumers.

top_gaps.title: Service Discovery
top_gaps.gap: No machine-readable API catalog for programmatic library discovery.
top_gaps.impact: Agent-based tools cannot programmatically discover Arrow's capabilities.

top_gaps.title: Programming Languages
top_gaps.gap: Python 3.8 minimum still supported (past EOL); pyupgrade targets py36.
top_gaps.impact: Maintains legacy compatibility burden; limits modern feature adoption.

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists — comprehensive RST documentation in docs/ directory, README.rst, CHANGELOG.rst, and Sphinx-generated API docs.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A knowledge agent that indexes Arrow's documentation, changelog, and source docstrings to answer developer questions about date/time handling patterns.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Documentation is already structured in RST format suitable for indexing. Could generate an interface description from the typed Python API.

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (GitHub Actions workflows for testing, linting, and PyPI release).
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that monitors CI status, triggers releases, checks test coverage trends, and manages Dependabot PRs.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: GitHub Actions API is already the control surface. Agent would need repository access token.

findings.title: No audit logging or release provenance
findings.description: No CloudTrail or equivalent audit logging. The PyPI publish workflow uses a long-lived API token with no artifact signing, no SLSA provenance, and no build attestation.
findings.gap: No audit trail for library build and release operations. No artifact signing or provenance attestation for published packages.
findings.recommendation: Adopt PyPI Trusted Publishers (OIDC-based publishing) to eliminate long-lived API tokens. Add Sigstore signing for release artifacts to create a verifiable audit trail.

findings.title: No distributed tracing instrumentation
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry SDK, no X-Ray instrumentation, no trace context propagation. Library operations are opaque in consumer application traces.
findings.gap: Libraries that perform significant computation can benefit from optional span creation. Arrow operations appear as opaque blocks in application traces.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding optional OpenTelemetry instrumentation (as an optional dependency) that creates spans for parsing and timezone conversion operations.

findings.title: No Python dependency vulnerability scanning
findings.description: Dependabot is configured for GitHub Actions only. No Python package vulnerability monitoring (pip-audit, Safety, Snyk). Vulnerable dependencies could be published to consumers.
findings.gap: No CVE scanning for python-dateutil, backports.zoneinfo, or test dependencies. Python packages in requirements/ are not monitored for vulnerabilities.
findings.recommendation: Add pip ecosystem to Dependabot configuration. Add pip-audit to CI pipeline for explicit vulnerability scanning.

findings.title: No machine-readable API catalog
findings.description: Library is discoverable via PyPI and ReadTheDocs but has no machine-readable API specification. Discoverability relies on documentation and type stubs.
findings.gap: No formal machine-readable API catalog or interface description beyond py.typed marker and docstrings. Agent-based tools cannot programmatically discover the library's full interface.
findings.recommendation: Consider generating a structured API reference via Sphinx autodoc JSON output that could serve as a machine-readable interface catalog for agent-based tool discovery.

findings.title: Python 3.8 minimum still supported (past EOL)
findings.description: Library supports Python 3.8-3.14 with modern tooling (pyproject.toml, Flit, type annotations, mypy strict). However, Python 3.8 reached EOL October 2024, and pyupgrade targets --py36-plus.
findings.gap: Maintaining Python 3.8 support limits adoption of modern features (walrus operator, pattern matching, stdlib zoneinfo) and requires backports.zoneinfo dependency.
findings.recommendation: Raise minimum Python version to 3.9+ to eliminate backports.zoneinfo dependency. Update pyupgrade target to --py39-plus.

findings.title: No formal deprecation policy
findings.description: Library uses semantic versioning (1.4.0) with CHANGELOG.rst and explicit __all__. However, no programmatic deprecation warnings or formal deprecation policy exists.
findings.gap: No explicit deprecation warnings (warnings.warn with DeprecationWarning) for API changes. No documented deprecation timeline or policy.
findings.recommendation: Add deprecation warnings for any API changes before removal. Document a formal deprecation policy (e.g., deprecated features removed after 2 minor versions).

findings.title: No dedicated SAST tool in pipeline
findings.description: CI includes flake8-bugbear, mypy strict, python-no-eval hook providing partial security coverage. However, no dedicated SAST tool (Bandit, Semgrep) and no dependency vulnerability scanning (pip-audit).
findings.gap: No dedicated SAST tool for security-specific analysis. The existing tools provide code quality but not focused security analysis.
findings.recommendation: Add Bandit (Python security linter) to pre-commit hooks or CI pipeline. Add pip-audit as a CI step for dependency vulnerability scanning.
